pairs.question: What is the Google & Kaggle AI Agents Intensive Course and when does it run?
pairs.answer: The Google & Kaggle AI Agents Intensive Course is a 5-day program designed to teach participants about AI agents, their architectures, tools, and best practices. It runs from November 10-14 and is open for registration until the course begins.

pairs.question: How can participants submit their writing challenge entries for the AI Agents Intensive Course?
pairs.answer: Participants can submit their writing challenge entries by publishing a post on DEV using the provided submission template. The post should include reflections on their learning journey, key takeaways, or a showcase of their capstone project. Entries must be submitted by December 14 at 11:59 PM PST.

pairs.question: What are the judging criteria for the Google & Kaggle AI Agents Writing Challenge?
pairs.answer: The writing challenge entries will be judged based on three criteria: Style and Presentation, Clarity, and Originality. A panel selected by DEV will evaluate the submissions to select one winner.

pairs.question: What prizes are available for the writing challenge winners of the AI Agents Intensive Course?
pairs.answer: The winner of the writing challenge will receive an exclusive Winner's Badge and a DEV++ Membership. All qualifying participants will receive a completion badge.

pairs.question: How can participants access the course materials if they register late for the Google & Kaggle AI Agents Intensive Course?
pairs.answer: Late registrants can access course materials on Kaggle’s Discord server and in the Learn Guide on Kaggle. However, they won’t receive earlier emails sent to early registrants.

pairs.question: What is the capstone project component of the Google & Kaggle AI Agents Intensive Course?
pairs.answer: The capstone project is a hands-on component where participants apply their skills to build either a simple AI agent or a sophisticated multi-agent system. Projects are made public and live on Kaggle from November 14-30.

pairs.question: Can participants start writing their challenge submission before completing the entire AI Agents Intensive Course?
pairs.answer: Yes, participants can start writing their challenge submissions from Day 1 of the course. Any learnings and reflections are welcome, even if they don’t complete the full course.
